How important are the character/wheel dupes gameplay-wise? As in, do they become a necessity at some point? Asking mostly since I see most of the tierlists on the game recommending you to get multiple copies of specific chars/wheels, but at the same time I've seen some people on the game's sub say that dupes should be your least priority and they mostly just make the skill rotation easier.
Depends
Copies 1-3 are generally the most relevant
some characters need breakpoints more than others, but they all give unique effects
copy 8 gives overexalt, which is generally a pretty big power boost but it's also generally not _needed_, though some awakeners want it more than others
4-7 and 9-15 are just stats
overexalt is the most you should really ever aim for if you don't plan to spend, the extras are never really worth it
its just icing
for wheels, three extra copies will max out the 'effect' stats
generally even for a good limited wheel three extra copies is all you want
getting all fifteen dupes for useful standard wheels is helpful, because then you can run two SSRs
but not really worth for the limiteds
most units are playable at e0, and yeah, having their dupes makes them play faster as in you get your skills/stacks/damage up faster
some other characters on the other hand need their dupes to unlock parts of their kit
like a unique function locked behind an enlighten
depends on the character
but as you go further in the story you'll see that the life and damage of monsters really ramps up

what makes lone seed a good posse?

Many of the other copy effects in this game (like thais) give the copied card exhaust and fleeting. Lone seed is only exhaust, so if you copy an important card that has retain with lone seed it will stay in hand at end of turn. The obvious example would be copying a ghelot flail before your burst turn.
